_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Assist in the construction of signatures which can be implemented on cyber defense network tools in response to new or observed threats within the NE or enclave.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting security assessments of data platforms and AI systems, including vulnerability identification, penetration testing coordination, and security architecture review.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-S-008 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-A-003 (SP, cosine: 0.389), SP-T-010 (SP, cosine: 0.381).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.5/10 (raw 0.251). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [assist, construction, cyber, defense, enclave, implemented] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, architecture, assessments, conducting, coordination, data] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.860 (P=0.860, R=0.860). Balanced P/R — the 27-word DCWF element and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.6%). "Assist in the construction of signatures which can be implemented on cyber defen" and "Skill in conducting security assessments of data platforms and AI systems, inclu" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.390 > STS 0.251 (gap +0.139). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 4.9%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [assist, construction, cyber, defense, enclave, implemented]. WIDAI terms: [ai, architecture, assessments, conducting, coordination, data].
